name: Umm Abdullah
title: Mother of Imam Baqir (AS)
shortDescription: The daughter of Imam Hasan (AS), wife of Imam Sajjad (AS), and mother of Imam Baqir (AS) who was present in Karbala and suffered captivity.
fullStory:
Umm Abdullah bint Hasan ibn Ali was a highly virtuous lady from the Bani Hashem family. She was the daughter of Imam Mujtaba (AS) and the wife of Imam Zayn al-Abidin (AS). The fruit of this blessed marriage was a child who later reached the position of Imamate: Imam Muhammad al-Baqir (AS).

Umm Abdullah, along with her ill husband (Imam Sajjad) and her young child (Imam Baqir), who was about four years old at the time, was present in the Karbala caravan. She witnessed the martyrdom of her dear uncle Imam Hussain (AS) and her brothers (Qasim, Abdullah, and Abu Bakr).

After Ashura, with a grieving heart but a firm resolve, she took on the nursing of her ill husband and the protection of her young child on the hard and exhausting route of captivity from Kufa to Sham.

Imam Baqir (AS) later described his noble mother, saying: 'My mother was among the truthful and righteous women; no woman like her in virtue could be found among the family of Imam Hasan (AS).'
quoteArabic: كانَتْ أُمِّي صِدِّيقَةً لَمْ تُدْرَكْ فِي آلِ الحَسَنِ امْرَأَةٌ مِثْلَها...
quote: (Imam Baqir's statement about his mother:) My mother was a very truthful woman; no woman like her in virtue could be found in Imam Hasan's family.
